name: High Fructose Corn Syrup
description: A cheap artificial sweetener linked to fatty liver disease and insulin resistance.

name: Sodium Benzoate
description: A common preservative that can form benzene (a carcinogen) when combined with vitamin C.

name: Aspartame
description: An artificial sweetener with controversial links to neurological effects.

name: Monosodium Glutamate
description: A flavor enhancer that may cause temporary adverse effects in sensitive individuals.

name: TBHQ
description: A synthetic preservative linked to organ toxicity and potential carcinogenic effects.

name: Red 40
description: A synthetic dye associated with hyperactivity in children and potential allergic reactions.

name: Soy Lecithin
description: A natural emulsifier derived from soybeans. Generally recognized as safe.

name: Citric Acid
description: A natural preservative found in citrus fruits. Generally safe.

name: Caffeine
description: A natural stimulant that affects the central nervous system.

name: Salt
description: Essential mineral; excessive intake linked to hypertension and kidney disease.

name: Disodium Inosinate
description: A flavor enhancer often used with MSG. May cause reactions in sensitive individuals.

name: Methylcellulose
description: A plant-derived thickener used as a binder in plant-based foods.

name: Natural Flavors
description: Natural flavor extracts. Generally safe but can be proprietary blends.
